name: Candler Pawn & Gun Shop
description_short: Candler Rd Pawn & Gun is a 40+ year-old Atlanta-area pawn shop offering collateral-based loans, buying, selling, and layaway services on firearms, jewelry, electronics, and other goods.
description_long:
Candler Rd Pawn & Gun has operated in the Decatur, Georgia area for over 40 years, establishing itself as a community fixture in the greater Atlanta region. The shop is located at the intersection of Glenwood Road and Candler Road and serves customers seeking quick access to cash through collateral-based lending, as well as those buying, selling, or trading used and new merchandise.

The company offers a full spectrum of pawn shop services: short-term loans using personal property as collateral, direct purchasing of items, retail sales of new and used goods, and layaway services. Their inventory spans firearms and accessories (both new and used), jewelry, electronics, lawn care equipment, scrap gold, and musical instruments. They also advertise the ability to special-order firearms and accessories not currently in stock. Pawn loans have a 30-day minimum repayment period with no stated maximum if renewed on time.

Candler Rd Pawn & Gun distinguishes itself through longevity and specialization in firearms alongside traditional pawn services. The 40+ year operating history suggests customer trust and stability. Their emphasis on both new firearm sales and pawn lending creates a dual-revenue model that combines retail and lending services in a single location, which is less common than pawn shops focused solely on collateral lending.

The pricing structure carries significant cost: loans are capped at 25% annual percentage rate plus late fees, with a $10 filing fee on the first month. A $100 loan over 30 days costs $135 total—a 35% upfront cost. This is higher than payday loans in many states but reflects standard pawn shop economics. The company's transparency about terms is commendable, though consumers should understand that pawned items can be forfeited if loans aren't repaid, and the APR compounds quickly on renewed loans.
pros: 40+ years of continuous operation in Atlanta area demonstrates business stability and community presence
Transparent loan disclosure showing specific APR (25% max), filing fees ($10), and worked example ($100 loan costs $135 at 30 days)
Diverse inventory categories (firearms, jewelry, electronics, gold, instruments, lawn equipment) reduces need to shop multiple locations
Offers both collateral-based lending AND retail sales of new firearms and accessories, plus special-order capability
Quick and easy loan process advertised with friendly staff, no stated maximum loan period if renewed on time
Serves as both pawn lender and buy/sell dealer, allowing customers to liquidate items or acquire goods beyond pawning
Layaway option provides alternative credit structure for customers unable to secure traditional financing
cons: Maximum 25% APR plus late fees is expensive compared to personal loans (8-36%) and payday alternatives under 36% APR
Worked example shows $100 loan costs $135 over 30 days (35% effective cost), making renewal cycles financially punitive
No maximum loan period if renewed means borrowers can become trapped in debt cycle with compounding interest
Collateral-based lending means pawned items can be forfeited, creating risk of losing personal property of sentimental or greater monetary value
No online application, payment, or account management mentioned; requires in-person visits for all transactions
best_for: Consumers with collateral (jewelry, firearms, electronics) who need immediate cash and cannot qualify for traditional bank loans
Atlanta-area firearms buyers seeking both retail new inventory and used gun selection in one location
Customers with flexible repayment timelines who can pay within 30 days to avoid renewal and compounding interest
People with no credit history or poor credit seeking non-credit-based lending secured only by collateral value
diagnosis: Candler Rd Pawn & Gun is best for Atlanta-area residents who need emergency cash immediately and have valuable collateral to pledge, particularly firearms or jewelry. The critical caveat is that the 25% APR plus fees creates expensive debt if loans are renewed repeatedly, and pawned items can be permanently lost if loans aren't repaid—this is a high-risk lending option and should only be used as a last resort for short-term cash needs, not ongoing borrowing.
services: Pawn loans (collateral-based lending with 30-day minimum period)
Buy used firearms, jewelry, electronics, lawn equipment, instruments, and scrap gold
Sell new firearms and firearm accessories
Sell used merchandise across multiple categories
Special order firearms and accessories not in current inventory
Pawn loan renewal (no stated maximum period if renewed on time)
Loan disclosure and APR disclosure at point of service
